title: Ambulance Care Assistant
how_to_become: Apply for vacancies on the NHS recruitment website to become an ambulance care assistant. There are no specific entry requirements, but the Scottish Ambulance Service will expect a good standard of education. A full UK driving licence including category D1 is required.

salary.entry_note: Ambulance care assistants start at £29,103 before tax. Trainees are paid £21,827, increasing to £29,103 on completion of the training programme.
salary.experienced_note: Ambulance care assistants can earn up to £31,409 before tax.

description: Ambulance care assistants provide a non-emergency transport service, taking elderly, sick, and disabled people to and from hospital appointments where medical or clinical support is needed during the journey.
role_overview: Working in the ambulance transport team, you'll provide high-quality care and safe transportation for non-emergency patients, driving patients from home to hospital for clinical appointments or between hospitals when a different level of care is needed. You'll also provide additional support during major incidents or large public events.

entry_requirements.overview: No specific entry requirements, but a good standard of education and a full UK driving licence including category D1 are required.

work_experience: Work placements and volunteering can provide healthcare experience and help when applying to university, college, or a new job with NHSScotland.

education_and_training.training_programme: An internal education programme lasting approximately 4 months. From the start date, trainees are employees of the Scottish Ambulance Service and enrolled in a 4-week clinical programme.
training_includes: Patient care for priority groups such as cancer, cardiology, and mental health patients
education_and_training.qualification_awarded: Certificate for Ambulance Patient Care: Non-Urgent Care Services

learning_and_development.overview: During your career, you must keep your skills and knowledge up to date. The Scottish Ambulance Service will provide training specific to your role.

career_progression.overview: With experience and training, you could move into more senior or emergency roles.

chunks.text: To become an ambulance care assistant, apply for vacancies on the NHS recruitment website. No specific entry qualifications are required, but a good standard of education and a full UK driving licence including category D1 are needed.

Ambulance care assistants start at £29,103 before tax. Trainees are paid £21,827, increasing to £29,103 on completion of the training programme. Experienced ambulance care assistants can earn up to £31,409 before tax.

Ambulance care assistants provide a non-emergency transport service, taking elderly, sick, and disabled people to and from hospital appointments where medical or clinical support is needed during the journey.

Ambulance care assistants provide high-quality care and safe transportation for non-emergency patients, driving them from home to hospital or between hospitals. They also provide additional support during major incidents or large public events.
No specific entry requirements are needed, but a good standard of education including English and Maths, and a full UK driving licence including category D1 are required.
Work placements and volunteering can provide healthcare experience and help when applying for a new job with NHSScotland.

Training is delivered through a 4-month internal Scottish Ambulance Service programme including classroom training, advanced safe driving, first aid, moving and handling, using automated defibrillators, patient care, and managing conflict. Trainees receive a Certificate for Ambulance Patient Care: Non-Urgent Care Services on completion.

With experience and training, ambulance care assistants can progress to team leader, supervisor, patient transport services controller, or apply for accident and emergency roles such as ambulance technician.
